description: Anatomical subregion vocabulary for LLM finding classification. Each canonical body region maps to mutually exclusive subregion options derived from standard radiology report section headings. Used by the summarization prompt builder to constrain the LLM's region assignments for extracted findings.
designPrinciples: Derived from actual radiology report section headings
8-15 mutually exclusive subregions per canonical region
Plain language labels recognizable to any radiologist
Every finding should fit exactly one subregion
Includes 'Other' as escape valve for edge cases

excludedCanonicalRegions.note: Mass, Soft, Bone, and Joint are generic canonical regions that apply across body areas. Their anatomy is incorporated as subregions within the specific canonical regions below (e.g., 'Bones and Marrow' within Arm, 'Joint Spaces' within Calf). These generic regions are excluded from this vocabulary because they rarely appear as primary study regions and their findings are better classified within the anatomical context of a specific body area.

Head.description: For CT Head, MR Brain, CT/MR Head studies

Face.description: For CT Face, CT/MR Sinuses, CT/MR Temporal Bones, CT/MR Orbits, Panorex studies

Neck.description: For CT Neck, MR Neck, CT Angiogram Neck studies

Spine.description: For general spine studies spanning multiple spinal segments (e.g., MR Whole Spine, CT Spine)

Cervical.description: For MR C-Spine, CT C-Spine, XR C-Spine studies

Thoracic.description: For MR T-Spine, CT T-Spine, XR T-Spine studies

Lumbar.description: For MR L-Spine, CT L-Spine, XR L-Spine studies

Chest.description: For CT Chest, XR Chest, CT Pulmonary Angiogram, CT Chest Angiogram studies

Breast.description: For Mammography, Breast MRI, Breast Ultrasound studies. Uses ACR BI-RADS structured reporting conventions.

Abdomen.description: For CT Abdomen, US Abdomen, MR Abdomen, CT Abdomen/Pelvis (abdomen portion) studies

Pelvis.description: For CT Pelvis, MR Pelvis, US Pelvis, CT Abdomen/Pelvis (pelvis portion) studies

Arm.description: For MR Shoulder, XR Shoulder, XR Humerus, US Shoulder studies

Forearm.description: For MR Elbow, XR Elbow, XR Forearm studies

Hand.description: For MR Wrist, XR Hand, XR Wrist, XR Fingers studies

Leg.description: For MR Hip, XR Hip, XR Femur, CT Lower Extremity studies

Calf.description: For MR Knee, XR Knee, XR Tibia/Fibula studies

Foot.description: For MR Ankle, XR Ankle, XR Foot, MR Foot studies

Whole.description: For PET/CT Whole Body, PET/CT Skull Base to Mid-Thigh, NM Bone Scan studies
